text.value: ADVISE FOR CONCERN: You have tried your best to comfort your child. In addition, I want to add that it is very important to look into the child's eyes, hold them gently, and speak to them all the time. Help the women to demonstrate again using the new techniques
How do you comfort your child when he/she is upset, crying, sad or angry"?

You have tried your best to distract the child from an unwanted behavior. In addition, I want to add that it is always good to draw/shift your child's attention to something positive. This is better for child development than scolding or hitting them. If a child does a good behavior, praise them so that they continue to do the good behavior. Help the women to demonstrate again using the new techniques.

ADVISE/CONCERN: Playing and talking to children is important for their healthy development, but it can be difficult to know what to do. A child who has no opportunities to play will not develop healthily. Children learn by seeing what others do, copying and practicing new things. Play is a great way to help children learn and practice new skills!

I want to discuss with you ways to correct the child when they are acting/doing an unwanted behavior. For example, maybe the child is trying to take a very hot cup of chai. This is bad, because the child might spill the cup and get burnt. Can you tell or show me how you would correct the child in this situation?
Caregiver reports or demonstrates interacting well?
Have you observed the caregiver comforting the child?
Have you observed the caregiver correcting the child?
Distracts child from unwanted actions with appropriate toy or activity.
Scolds child or responds harshly to child.
How did the caregiver correct the child?

Ask : Do you play with your child? Can you show or tell me what types of ways you play with your child? Can you show or tell me how you like to talk or sing with them or how you try to get them to smile? Observe whether caregiver reports or demonstrates ways to plays , talks, sings with child, e.g. with toy objects or word games, or other, appropriate for developmental stage.
PRAISE: I can see that you do a very good job comforting the child and showing them love.

PRAISE the caregiver for doing a good job correcting the child in a positive way. Encourage caregiver to praise the child for all good behavior. I can see that you do a very good job distracting your child from unwanted behaviors. It is always good to draw/shift your child's attention to something positive. This is better for child development than scolding or hitting them. If a child does a good behavior, praise them so that they continue to do the good behavior.
PRAISE the way caregiver demonstrated playing with the child for their healthy development.
Assess Caregiver and Child Interactions and Behavior

ADVISE FOR CONCERN You have tried your best to comfort your child. In addition, I want to add that it is very important to look into the child's eyes, hold them gently, and speak to them all the time. Help the women to demonstrate again using the new techniques
Have you observed the caregiver comforting the child?
Did the caregiver comfort the child by looking into child's eyes, talking softly to child, gently touching or holding the child?
I would like to talk about how you comfort the child and show them love. Let's pretend that your child is disturbed right now. Can you tell me what you would do to comfort them, and show them love?
For the following questions, observe interaction between caregiver and child if possible. If have not observed so far during visit, ask caregiver to demonstrate.

ADVISE on how to improve interactions between caregiver and child SAY: Playing and communicating with baby's is important to support healthy development, but it can be difficulty to know what to do. A baby who does not have opportunities to play or exchange sounds and smiles with caregivers will not develop healthily. In a moment, I am going to share some other ideas about playing and communicating with your baby
Does caregiver shows or describes good interactions with the baby by talking, singing, stroking, smiling, gently playing with toy?
Can you tell me what kinds of playing, talking and singing you do with your baby, or can you show me?
Did the caregiver mention ways to comfort the child?
Observe the caregiver describes interactions with the baby
PRAISE the caregiver for doing a good job comforting the child and showing them love.
PRAISE mother for playing well with baby. It is great to see you interacting with your baby with talking and play! I am going to share some other ideas about playing and communicating with your baby
Do you talk or sing to your baby or try to get your baby to smile?
Assess Caregiver and Child Interactions and Behavior

Praise father for making the time to join a visit. Discuss with father: Your child's intelligence grows rapidly during the first 3 years of life and the experiences they have during this time set the path for the rest of their live. The more you can do to help them develop their brains during this time, the more intelligent and happier they will be in school and later in life. A child's family can help by: Keeping your child healthy with immunizations and seeking care at a clinic quickly if your child is sick. In JNA we will make sure your child has all their immunizations and help you to learn to recognize signs of sickness. Giving your child healthy foods. Regular meals with different types of food are important to help the child's body and brain to grow well. In Jamii ni Afya we will check for problems with growth, and talk about a healthy, varied diet. Boosting brain growth by spending time each day Playing, Talking and Praising your child. Positive interactions young infants and children have with family members prepares them for later success in school and life. On the other hand, a lot of fighting, conflict, stress or harsh discipline can create difficulties for a child's development. In JNA visits we will practice some ways to play, talk and praise your child at different ages.

ASK: Ask caregivers if they have any other specific questions about how to help and take care of their child who has a delay in development. Ask them what is most difficult for them day to day. Ask them if they have any goals for things they want to work with their child. Find more information to counsel about caring for children with delays here: Link to disability reference. Also, due to this concern with your child's development, at the end of this visit I am going to refer you to the nearest health facility so you can ask them about this concern with your child's development and get additional health care for your child. SCREEN the child for serious delays in development or disability using the following 11 Question Screen. Any yes answer indicates a risk for serious delay or development. Advise the mother that because she expressed a concern about her child being slow to learn, you are going to ask some more questions to help to understand in which ways your child might have a delay in developing that might need more evaluation. I want to ask you some other specific questions about your child's development.
12 months up to 2 years: Walking on own Talks, by 2 years old begins to make very short sentences Begins to form questions Eats on own Sleep throughout the night
2 years and 3 years: Expresses desires, needs, questions, etc. forming full thoughts in sentences and curious about world around them. Runs and jumps.
Does your child: 3 to 6 months: Responds to sound Looks and and follows object with eyes Interested in faces Starts to reach for objects
4 to 5 years: Tells stories, knows some colors and letter, and begins to count Others can understand when child speaks
6 months up to 9 months: Smiles back when you smile at child Alerts caregiver when hungry Coos, makes noises to communicate Sits up on own Recognizes and responds to caregivers and colors
9 months up to 12 months: Begins to babble and form first words Responds to caregivers verbally and physically Crawls Stands on own Walking on own or with assistance
Having a child who is slow to learn can be upsetting and stressful for everyone in the family.
Do you have any concerns about how the child sees or hears?
Do you or the caregiver have any concerns that child is not responding or seems slow?

Have a conversation with the caregiver including: A child with a delay can make progress and learn It is important the child receives regular health care like immunizations and visits to clinic when sick. A child with delays may need more support for feeding but should be given a healthy and regular meals. A child with delays may need more help with sitting, bathing and toileting. Spending extra time playing and communicating with your child can help them develop new abilities. Include your child in your day to day activities like going to the store or eating meals with the family. Try to notice and encourage abilities the child has .
What age matches/compares with the development of the child?
To be answered by the CHV
If father joined the visit, Ask: Is this is the first visit you have joined?
If father not joined, Ask if father has ever joined in a visit? If no, ask the mother to try to arrange for father to join in a visit in the future.

The child's age is months. Ask the caregiver if the child can do usual behaviors for children at this age. If not, ask about behaviors in younger age range. If there is a concern about delay in development, start asking about milestones at younger ages and work up to milestones at older ages.
Use this age range to choose and coach on play and communication activities to best match the developmental level of the child.
**_Check Developmental Milestones to help target play and communication activities closest to child's developmental stage_**
Compared with other children of the same age, does your child appear in any way mentally backward, dull or slow?
Can your child name at least one object (for example, an animal, a toy, a cup, a spoon)?
Does your child learn to do things like other children his/her age?
Compared with other children, does your child have any serious delay in sitting, standing or walking?
Does your child have difficulty hearing? (Uses hearing aid, hears with difficulty, completely deaf)
Compared with other children, does your child have difficulty seeing, either in the daytime or at night?
Does your child have difficulty in walking or moving his/her arms, or does he/she have weakness, and/or stiffness in the arms or legs?
Does your child speak at all (can he/she make him or herself understood in words; can say any recognizable words)?
Does your child sometimes have fits, becomes rigid, or lose consciousness?
Is your child's speech in any way different from normal (not clear enough to be understood by people other than the immediate family)?
When you tell your child to do something, does he/she seem to understand what you are saying?

Advise caregiver that you would like to talk about the child's behaviors and development and observe how they interact together. Explain that the way the child grows and learns depends very much on the behavior that caregivers show to the child. Are there any other family members or caregivers who could join us for a discussion about your child's development? In particular, encourage fathers and grandmothers to join if available. Welcome them to join the discussion. Also ask the mother to have her child join or be nearby. Welcome caregivers and family members and encourage them to join the discussion.
Care for child development (CCD): Assess Development and Coach on Play and Communication
Has the child recently had any convulsions?
Your child has a danger sign. This is an URGENT and super serious emergency. You should take the child IMMEDIATELY to the nearest facility. If it is closed, you need to go to the closest hospital.
I'm now going to screen your child for danger signs. Has your child exhibited any of these symptoms within the past 24 hours?
Is the child having difficulty or fast breathing (Two counts of 60 breaths or more in one minute)?
Does the child have fever or high temperature ?
Is the child lethargic or unconscious?
Does the child have severe diarrhea? (More than 10 loose, watery stools in 24 hours)

Now you are at the stage of screening danger signs for this child. Please avoid touching the child in order to avoid the possibility of corona virus transmission. In case you need to physically examine the child, please ask the client to do so on your behalf.
Is the child having cold or low temperature?
Is the child able to drink or breastfeed?
Is the child able to eat and drink?
Is the child able to breastfeed?
Does the child have mild diarrhea? (Not more than 10 loose, watery stools in 24 hours)
A danger sign has been identified. Advise the mother to go to any nearby health facility within the next 24 hours so a doctor can look at the baby and help.
Does the child have skin boils ?
No danger signs were identified in your child, but if the child develops any of the signs we discussed, you should go the health facility immediately.

I'd like to discuss disposal of children's feces with you. A child's stool can be dangerous and can cause diseases.
Where does your child defecate? Where do you dispose your child feces?
Do you have a latrine in your household?
Using latrines can help prevent the spread of disease. If possible, you should have a latrine in your home.

Explain to the mother the dangers of child stool and safe disposal: The safest way to dispose of a child's feces is to help the child use a toilet or latrine or, for very young children, to put or rinse their feces into a toilet or latrine. There is evidence that children's feces could be riskier than adults' feces. Children's feces should be treated with the same concern as adult feces, using safe disposal methods that ensure separation from human contact and household contamination.

Hello mom? As we mentioned during our previous visits that I will come to visit you to find out the baby's health and well-being, as well as to discuss with you and your family about your child's care and early childhood development. I know you know how to take care of your baby and raise him or her properly, but this is just an additional education due to an increase various health problem that can undermine the health and well-being of your child/children.
Name: Age: years, months, days Contact person: House number: Kitongoji:

Instructions for CHV: Select 1 play and 1 communication activity from the Play and Communication Guide at the developmental stage of the child. i. Show the parents the pictures and explain each activity. ii. Let caregivers try the activities. Observe the interaction and help caregivers be sensitive and responsive to child's cues. Aim to help the caregiver and child have a successful "two-way" interaction with caregiver observing, listening and responding to child. iii. If child is not able to do the activity choose from younger age range or suggest ways to make the play simpler. If child is at end of age range and can easily do activity, suggest ways to help the child learn something new or choose activity from next older age range iv. Encourage the caregiver to engage in play and communication activities daily.

Children learn by seeing what others do, copying and practicing new things. Play is a great way to help children learn and practice new skills! The more time we can spend playing with our children, the more information is given to their brain to help it grow. It also helps them physically. Let the child show you how they want to play, and follow their interests. Talking to our children, responding to their sounds and gestures, and helping them learn to name things helps their brain to grow and helps them learn to speak.
Tell caregiver you are going to demonstrate then practice some play and communication activities for her to continue to do at home.
Ask your child simple questions. Respond to your child's attempts to talk. Show and talk about nature, pictures, and things.

Encourage your child to talk and answer your child's questions. Teach your child stories, songs and games. Talk about pictures or books. Children will learn words if you draw something then repeat the word. ADVISE the caregiver that they can draw their own child-friendly picture books, they don't need to buy in the shop. Example toy: book with pictures, material to draw on, ball, swing, bicycle.
Smile and laugh with your child. Talk to your child. Get a conversation going by copying your child's sounds and gestures

Encourage your child to count, compare and match items around you. Give opportunities to draw. Help them learn letters and numbers. Encourage her to help you with household activities. Provide opportunities to run, jump and climb.
Respond to your child's sounds and interests. Call the child's name, and see your child respond.
Tell your child the names of things and people. Show your child how to say things with hands, like 'bye bye.' Sample toy: doll.

Encourage your child to count, compare and match items around you. Give opportunities to draw. Help them learn letters and numbers. Encourage her to help you with household activities. Provide opportunities to run, jump and climb
Help your child count, name and compare things. Make simple toys for your child. Sample toys: Objects of different colors and shapes to sort, sticks.
Give your child clean, safe household things to handle, bang, and drop. Sample toys: containers with lids, metal pot and spoon.
Hide a child's favorite toy under a cloth or box. See if the child can find it. Play peek-a-boo.
Give your child things to stack up, and to put into containers and take out. Sample toys: Nesting and stacking objects, container and clothes clips.
Some good ways to communicate with your child are:
At this age, some good ways to play with your child are:
Demonstrating and Coaching Play and Communication Activities

SAY: I'd like to enroll into infant and child services. I'll do a first check-up on their health today, and them return at important times as they grow up to make sure they are healthy and well. Is that OK?
Greet the family and invite others to join the conversation as appropriate.

As a CHV, you are responsible for making sure that you are providing the community with health education and services while trying to take precautions to stop the transmission of Corona virus to one another. Therefore, make sure you consider the following while you conduct the visits: • Wash your hands with soap/sanitizer as many time as you can while at home, once you are at the client's house and when you leave. • Keep a 2 meters distance while talking to your clients. • Don't shake hands with your clients. • Avoid group gatherings. • Provide the service at the open space and if it should be inside, make sure the windows and doors are open. • Cover your mouth with a towel or elbow when you sneeze or cough. • Do not go for a visit if you have symptoms of fever, cough or frequent sneezing. • Please remember to do the covid education visit before or after submitting this form.
Precautionary measures when visiting a client

Explain the following regarding the importance of breastfeeding a small baby at least every 2-3 hours: Breast milk is the best food for a small baby as it protects the baby against infections A small baby needs to be fed more often because they can only take very small amounts of breast milk at a time If a baby is sleeping for longer than 2 hours, wake the baby up by smooth touch on soles such as palms and foot soles to breastfeed, even during the night
Based on your observation and the mother's explanation, does the mother breastfeed well?
How often do you breastfeed your baby, including night and day time?

How is the baby being kept warm? Praise the family if the baby is kept well wrapped and close to the mother, and is bathed with warm water and dried and wrapped immediately. If not, try to solve any problems. Refer to page 10 of Bango Kitita for more information.
How is it going? What positions are you using? How frequently do you breastfeed? How long will the baby feed at one time? LISTEN: Allow mother to explain her experience breastfeeding.

I'd like to talk to you more about breastfeeding.
ASK: have you ever breastfed?
Discuss with the mother why she is not exclusively breastfeeding. Advise her and problem solve with her.
Cannot select "None of the above" and another option.
Did the child drink or eat any of the following yesterday during the day or the night?
Do you have any concerns you wish to discuss? Discuss concerns with mother.

Discuss with the mother. Advise her and problem solve with her. Let's practice the proper attachment of the baby during breastfeeding. Could you please show me how you attach the baby? Ask her to touch the baby's lips with her nipple, wait until the baby's mouth is opening wide, and move the baby quickly onto her breast, aiming his lower lip below the nipple.Ask the mother to show you how to position the baby during breast feeding.
Discuss with the mother why she is not frequently breastfeeding. Advise her and problem solve with her.

If possible, ask the mother to breastfeed and observe. If not possible, ask questions below rather than observing.WASH your hands. OBSERVE the mother breastfeeding. Try to UNDERSTAND the situation.Do you notice any problems with breastfeeding? Provide guidance based on what you observe. Try to solve any problems you identify or the mother describes.
Praise the family if the baby is getting only breast milk.

Start the discussion by explaining/defining a "small" baby: A small baby is a baby who is born with less than 2 kg and is considered to be of low birth weight. Small babies may have more health issues than other babies. It is important to look for danger signs, keep them warm, and feed them often to keep them healthy. We are going to learn the issues small babies may face and how we can take care of them.
How long after birth did the mother first put the child to the breast?
Educate the mother about the proper positioning and attachment of the baby during breastfeeding. Refer to page numbers 16 and 17 of Bango Kitita.

Now you are at the stage of coaching the mother how she should position her child toward effective breast feeding. This activity sometimes needs your direct intervention and perhaps you may need to touch the child. However for this interim period, please stop direct intervention and avoid touching the child as a precautionary measure to avoid the spread of COVID-19. Instead, please direct the mother what to do while you observe and provide guidance.
Is the positioning and attachment ok? This means the chin is touching the breast, mouth wide open, lower lip turned outward, more areola visible above than below the mouth.

Help the mother to: Sit or lie comfortably. Support the baby's whole body, not just the neck and shoulders. Ensure baby's head and body are in a straight line. Have the baby face the breast. The baby's nose is opposite her nipple. Hold the breast and touch the baby's cheek with the nipple, so baby turns and opens mouth. Fix the baby's mouth on the nipple. Make sure baby is in a comfortable position to feed. Touch the baby's lips with her nipple.
Congratulate mother for breastfeeding in a right position.

Help the mother to improve positioning and attachment. If baby is too sleepy to feed, try to wake him by rubbing body, arms, or legs. If baby is still not feeding from the breast after one hour, squeeze the first, yellow fluid from the breast into a clean cup or spoon. Feed this to baby often and in small amounts, making sure baby can swallow it. Wait until the baby's mouth is opened wide Move the baby quickly onto her breast, aiming the infant's lower lip well below the nipple. If breast engorgement, express a small amount of breast milk before starting breastfeeding to soften nipple area so that it is easier for the baby to attach.
Praise the mother and advise her to continue doing the same.

Hygiene is very important during complimentary feeding, advise this to the mother: Good hygiene is important to avoid diarrhea Use a clean spoon or cup to give foods or liquids to your baby. Wash your hands with soap and running water frequently.
Hygiene is very important during complimentary feeding, advise this to the mother: Good hygiene is important to avoid diarrhea. Wash your hands with soap and running water frequently.

Let's talk about food Hygiene: Good hygiene is important to avoid diarrhea. Use a clean spoon or cup to give foods or liquids to your baby. Wash your hands with soap and running water frequently, especially before feeding your children.
If your baby is no longer being breastfed, please ensure that they are receiving a healthy, nutritious diet.
Continue breastfeeding your baby on demand both day and night. This will maintain health and strength as breast milk continues to be the most important part of your baby's diet.

Try to cook a variety of food for the baby: Each week you can add one new food to your child's diet. Feed your baby complementary foods 2 times a day. Give 2 to 3 tastes at each feed. Breastmilk should be your baby's main food at this age. Continue to breastfeed on demand. Drinking plain, clean water is a good habit to the baby's health. Use a separate plate to feed the baby.

Your baby's umbilical cord stump dries out and eventually falls off - usually within one to two weeks after birth. In the meantime, treat the area gently: Keep the stump dry.Expose the stump to air to help dry out the base. Keep the front of your baby's diaper folded down to avoid covering the stump. DO NOT apply any substance to the cord.It may be dangerous to your baby to apply any substances to his or her cord. Stick with sponge baths.While there's no harm in getting the stump wet, sponge baths might make it easier to keep the stump dry. Let the stump fall off on its own.Resist the temptation to pull off the stump yourself.

[Days 0-7:]
<span style="display:block;padding-left:1em">Talk and sing to the baby <span style="display:block;padding-left:1em">Provide ways for your baby to see, hear, move arms and legs freely, and touch you <span style="display:block;padding-left:1em">Smile at your baby <span style="display:block;padding-left:1em">Touch and hold the infant with love

[Days 8 to month 6]: PLAY: Provide ways for your child to see, hear, feel, move freely, and touch you. Slowly move colourful things for your child to see and reach for. Sample toys: shaker rattle, big ring on a string. COMMUNICATE: Smile and laugh with your child. Talk to your child. Get a conversation going by copying your child's sounds or gestures.

You and your partner/ husband or family members are responsible for the infant's brain and mind development, and these early months and years should be used for his/her growth.
A newborn baby can see things, feel things, and can respond to the voices and sounds she or he hears. A baby can tell us she or he is hungry or unhappy by the sounds he makes. A baby loves to see faces of her or his mum and dad (or other important caregiver). [see translation in EFFECTS job aids]
The more opportunities we give our babies to explore the world (by playing, practicing new skills, seeing new things and copying what others do), the more information is given to the brain.
For your newborn baby, you can:
Is the birthweight written on the child card?
This question tries to identify if small baby care is necessary
Enter a number between 0.5 and 7 KG
Enter brithweight of the child, in KG
Approximately how large was the baby at birth?
On the way to the health facility
Look at child card and/or KMC booklet for next scheduled follow-up visit. Advise mother to return to the facility for scheduled follow up visits
ASK: Have you received small baby services at the hospital?
This is a normal size baby and needs normal care. You will learn about care for the normal baby in later sessions.
This child is a small baby and extra care and attention is needed from both CHV and family. We will talk about some of the special care your baby needs.
is doing well and doesn't need to go to the health facility urgently at this time. You'll return for another checkup when it's time for the next visit.
To address an urgent child danger sign.
To address a child danger sign.
To address issues around learning and child development.
To address an urgent neonatal danger sign.
needs to go to the health facility for the following reasons:
To address a neonatal danger sign.
To receive specialized care for small babies.
You've declined to consent for to receive community health services. He/She won't receive any more visits from me.

Encourage the parent to take to the facility as soon as possible to receive care. Let them know that you'll return after 3 days to confirm that they were able to receive the proper care at the facility.
Now I would like to review your child's health card to make sure they have received all their vaccines so far. Please hand me your child's health card.
Ask mother to make sure facility staff records vaccinations on Child Health Card.
Before asking for the RCH card, please make sure you wash your hands with soap and please ask your client to wash their hands before handing over the card to you.
Please invite your husband and family so we can explain the importance of vaccinations.
Now I would like to review your child's health card to make sure they have received all their vaccines so far. Do you have your child's health card?

At this stage, your child is supposed to get the vaccine. Make sure you go to the facility to seek the vaccine for your child. In case you go and don't find one because of un avoided conditions, make sure you return once again so that your kid could be vaccinated. This is really important for your child's health.
Vaccine date should be between the child's birthdate and today.
Enter the date that the child received the BCG (TB) vaccine
Vaccine date should be between the child's birthdate and today.

Normally, when an infant receives BCG vaccination, a vaccine mark should be visible on the right arm after 3 months. If the vaccine mark is not visible, then advice the mother to take her child to the health facility to receive another BCG vaccination.
Vaccine date should be between the child's birthdate and today.
Enter the date that the child received the PCV 1 (Penumococcal , Pneumonia, Week 6) vaccine
Vaccine date should be between the child's birthdate and today.

Vaccine date should be between the child's birthdate and today.

All children under 5 are eligible for all vaccines, even if their vaccine dates are overdue. Please take your child to the health facility as soon as possible for vaccination.
Congratulate the mother and remind her of the next vaccination date according to the health card. Remind her how important it is that her child takes ALL the vaccines on time.

If your baby cries for more than 3 hours or if you are worried about how he looks or feels, go to the health facility immediately. Serious bad reactions very rare, but can happen occasionally as with any medicine.

After getting a vaccine, there is a small chance your baby may get a fever, rash, or a sore arm or leg where the vaccine was given. Give your child plenty of breastmilk. Usually this is mild and not a problem. If it doesn't go away quickly or if you have any concerns, go to the health facility and follow the advice they give you.
Provide counseling message around importance of vaccinations and refer to facility.
Give advice about the facility vaccination schedule and give an update on the stock availability on the vaccination. Work to ensure that the mother goes on the correct day.
If necessary, remind mother that all the vaccines are important to fully protect her child from diseases like diarrhea, polio, measles, Tuberculosis, pneumonia, and others.
LOOK at the health card. Are all vaccinations up to date?
That facility asked me to return on a different date
The baby is too old to receive that vaccination
Why did the child not receive the vaccine?

The child is at risk for malnutrition. SAY: Your child is in need of special care and attention to keep from getting malnourished. During the next visit, I will continue discussing best nutritional practices with you.

Now you are at the stage of screening danger signs for this child. Please avoid touching the child in order to avoid the possibility of corona virus transmission. In case you need to physically examine the child, please ask the client to do so on your behalf.
Your baby has a danger sign. This is an URGENT and super serious emergency. You should take the baby IMMEDIATELY to the nearest facility. If it is closed, you need to go to the closest hospital.
Is the baby blue in color?
Educate the mother on how to identify neonatal danger signs and the importance of seeking medical assistance at the health facility as soon as possible. Please refer to bango kitita, page number 18.

I'm now going to screen your baby for neonatal danger signs. If your child has danger signs, I shall refer him/her to a nearest health facility so that they can receive the appropriate medical attention. Has your baby exhibited any of these symptoms within the past 24 hours?
Is the baby having difficulty or fast breathing (Two counts of 60 breaths or more in one minute)?
Is the baby excessively crying or restlessness for more than one hour, non-stop?
Are the baby's eyes draining pus?
Does the baby have fever or high temperature?
Has the baby not been able to feed since birth, or stopped feeding well?
Is the baby not crying or not breathing normally?
Does the baby have severe diarrhea? (More than 10 loose, watery stools in 24 hours)
Does the baby have skin boils?
Does the baby have abdominal distension?
Does the baby have a bulging anterior fontanel?
Is the baby feeling cold or has low temperature?
Does the baby have congenital abnormality?
Is the baby's cord red, bleeding, or draining pus?
Does the baby have mild diarrhea? (Not more than 10 loose, watery stools in 24 hours)
Is the baby experiencing eye discharge or swollen eyes?
Is the baby only moving when stimulated?
Is the baby not passing stool for a day?
Is the baby not passing urine for a day?
Is the baby having skin rash?
Does the baby have yellow or pale palms and soles (hands and feet), and lips? Soles should be red/pink.
No danger signs were identified in your baby, but if the baby develops any of the signs we discussed, you should go the health facility immediately.
A danger sign has been identified. Advise the mother to go to any nearby health facility within the next 24 hours so a doctor can look at the baby and help.

If you've been providing pregnancy services to 's mother and haven't already finished the postpartum visit, you should return to conduct the postpartum visit after three days when you return for 's referral follow up visit.

Many people may face challenges when it comes to playing and communicating with their child to stimulate child development. I want to talk to you about how often you are playing, how you are able to play with your child and some common challenges parents face.
Are there concerns that family environment stressful with a lot of arguments?

Do you ever leave your child left alone for more than an hour?
Named, counted, or drew things for the child
Read books or looked at picture books
Sang to or with the child, including lullabies
Took the child outside the home

In the past three days, did the mother or any household member age 15 or over engage in any of the following activities with the child?
Sometimes adults taking care of children have to leave the house to go shopping, wash clothes, or for other reasons and have to leave young children.
How many children's books or picture books or cards do you have in your home? This can be any written material with pictures
Does the child play with homemade toys, such as rattles, dolls, cars, or other toys made at home?
Does the child play with household objects such as bowls or pots, or objects found outside, such as sticks, rocks, animal shells or leaves?

If you have concerns that child is not safe due to harsh or physical discipline, refer immediately to the Shehia Women and Children Coordinator or Sheha, who will help you contact the correct authorities or individual. You can also refer to the nearby police station or one stop centre.
Are you concerned about feeling too stressed or burdened to play and communicate with your child?

1. Listen to the caregivers feeling and help them identify a key person who can share their feelings and help them with their child. 2. Encourage the caregiver and other family members to support each other in playing and communicating with the child 3. Build their confidence by demonstrating their ability to carry out a simple activity.
Are you concerned about feeling that you don't have enough time to play and communicate?

Finding enough time is a common problem that many mothers and caregivers face. One strategy you may try, is to include the child with you in your daily activities, finding short moments throughout the day to talk and play. For example, a child may play with pots while you are cooking or sing together while you are cleaning. If you are feeling overwhelmed, try to get more help with work from other members of your family. Encourage other members of your household to play with your child too.

•Harsh child discipline will not support healthy development and can be harmful for the child. •Correcting and teaching the child in a positive manner is more effective and helps the child develop good social and emotional skills. •Praise the child for good behavior. •Use other strategies like distraction, giving choices, gentle correction if your child is not behaving well.

Discuss with caregiver that they should try to Identify at least one person (over 10 years old) who can care for the child regularly and give the child love and attention. Tell parents that it can be challenging for a child to get used to another caregiver at first, but that gradually, they can become accustomed to a new person.

Counsel on the harms that come from a stressful environment: •Children take their cues from their parents. Try to control your own stress and anger. •Keep children out of the argument. •Reassure your children that the argument is not their fault and that you love them. does CHV have concerns that the child is not safe due to a stressful home environment. Y/N If Yes, refer immediately to the Shehia Women and Children Coordinator or Sheha, who will help you contact the correct authorities or individual. You can also refer to the nearby police station or one stop centre.
Thank family for talking about these issues with me today. Remind family to practice some play and communication activities every day. Let family know when you will return.

Reassure caregiver that almost anything can be a toy and it's not necessary to spend lots of money. You can use any household objects that are clean and safe and appropriate to the child's age. Demonstrate how the caregiver can use local materials fabric/kitambaa or other household items to make toys or draw pictures to make books for the child. Show examples appropriate to the child's age. If the children are older, encourage the caregiver to develop no/low cost toys with their children.
How many total hours did the father/partner/male household head spend playing with the child in the last 3 days?
How many total hours did you or any household member over 15 spend playing with the child in the last three days?
Who engages in these activities with the child?
Help Families Identify and Solve Potential Problems

Your baby's umbilical cord stump dries out and eventually falls off - usually within one to two weeks after birth. In the meantime, treat the area gently: Keep the stump dry.Expose the stump to air to help dry out the base. Keep the front of your baby's diaper folded down to avoid covering the stump.DO NOT apply any substance to the cord._It may be dangerous to your baby to apply any substances to his or her cord.Stick with sponge baths.While there's no harm in getting the stump wet, sponge baths might make it easier to keep the stump dryLet the stump fall off on its own.Resist the temptation to pull off the stump yourself

Advise the mother on maintaining good hygiene, including washing her hands often with soap and water. Explain that preventing infections is even more important for the small baby than the normal baby as the small baby is more prone to infections.

Kangaroo Mother Care is a method of care for small babies in order to keep the baby warm. The method involves infants being carried with skin-to-skin contact.
Emphasize that anyone can do KMC.

Review the following steps in placing the baby skin-to-skin, and observe the mother as she practices KMC:
<span style="display:block;padding-left:1em">Undress the baby except for a nappy, hat and socks. <span style="display:block;padding-left:1em">Place the baby upright between the caregiver's breasts or chest with the baby's chest. <span style="display:block;padding-left:1em">Put the legs of the baby along the mother's ribs and turn his or her head to one side. <span style="display:block;padding-left:1em">Secure the baby with a special blouse or cloth tied around the mother and the baby. <span style="display:block;padding-left:1em">The mother can then wear a shirt, sweater or shawl if she wants. <span style="display:block;padding-left:1em">Ask the mother to breastfeed the baby as often as the baby wants but at least every 2 hours. <span style="display:block;padding-left:1em">It is very important that the baby receives the skin to skin contact for 20 hours every day

Do you know why small babies need to stay extra warm in the first days of life?
<span style="display:block;padding-left:1em">Small babies have less fat than larger babies, so low body temperature can be a major issue. Make sure your baby is dry and warm all the time. <span style="display:block;padding-left:1em">Kangaroo mother care (KMC) is an excellent way for mothers to keep these babies warm at all times. The skin-to-skin contact of KMC helps keep the baby's body temperature normal.

SAY: You and your partner/ husband or family members are responsible for the infant's brain and mind development, and these early months and years should be used for his/her growth.
A newborn baby can see things, feel things, and can respond to the voices and sounds she or he hears. A baby can tell us she or he is hungry or unhappy by the sounds he makes. A baby loves to see faces of her or his mum and dad (or other important caregiver). [see translation in EFFECTS job aids]
The more opportunities we give our babies to explore the world (by playing, practicing new skills, seeing new things and copying what others do), the more information is given to the brain.
For your newborn baby, you can:
[Days 0-7]: Talk and sing to the baby. Provide ways for your baby to see, hear, move arms and legs freely, and touch you. Smile at your baby. Touch and hold the infant with love.

[Days 8 to month 6]: PLAY: Provide ways for your child to see, hear, feel, move freely, and touch you. Slowly move colourful things for your child to see and reach for. Sample toys: shaker rattle, big ring on a string. COMMUNICATE: Smile and laugh with your child. Talk to your child. Get a conversation going by copying your child's sounds or gestures.

Understanding Cholera Ask the family: • What do you understand about cholera? • What are the symptoms of cholera? • How cholera is transmitted? • Who is at most risk for Cholera? • Can Cholera be prevented? Use Bango Kitita to explain more about Cholera based on the answers above.

USHAURI: Umejaribu uwezavyo kumfariji mtoto wako. Kwa kuongezea, nataka kuongeza kwamba ni muhimu sana kutazama macho ya mtoto, kumshika kwa upole, na kuzungumza naye kila wakati. Wasaidie wanawake kuonyesha tena kwa kutumia mbinu mpya.
Je, unamfariji vipi mtoto wako anapokasirika, anapolia,anakuwa na huzuni au hasira"?

MSHAURI JUU YA WASIWASI: Umejaribu uwezavyo kumkatasha mtoto kutoka kwa tabia isiyofaha. Kwa kuongeza, nataka kuongeza kwamba daima ni nzuri kuteka / kuhamisha mawazo ya mtoto wako kwa kitu chanya. Hii ni bora kwa ukuaji wa mtoto kuliko kuwakemea au kuwapiga. Ikiwa mtoto ana tabia nzuri, msifu ili aendelee kufanya tabia hiyo nzuri." Wasaidie wanawake kuonyesha tena kwa kutumia mbinu mpya.

MSHAURI: : Ni vizuri sana kuona unacheza na kuwasiliana na mtoto wako ili kusaidia maendeleo ya kiafya. Mtoto anajifunza kwa kuona wanachofanya wengine, kuiga na kufanyia mazoezi vitu vipya. Kucheza ni njia nzuri ya wao kujifunza

Naomba kujadiliana na wewe njia mzuri ya kuwarekebisha watoto wakiwa wamefanya makosa, kwa mfano jaalia mtoto anataka kuchukua kikombe cha chai moto, hili ni kosa, kwasababu huenda chai ikamwagikia akaungua. Je unaweza kunionesha ni vipi unaweza kumrekebisha mtoto katika hali hii?
Je, mlezi anaonyesha kucheza na mtoto wake kwaajili ya maendeleo ya ki afya?
Je, umemuona mlezi akimrekebisha mtoto wake?
Muapushe mtoto na vitendo visivyostahiki kwa kutumia vifaa vya kuchezea
Anamjibu mtoto kwa hasira na kumkaripia.

Uliza : Je, huwa unacheza na mtoto wako? Unaweza kunionesha au kuniambia aina gani za njia unazotumia kucheza na mtoto wako? Unaweza kunionesha au kuniambia jinsi unavyopenda kuongea na kuimba nae au jinsi unavyo jaribu kumfanya atabasamu? Chunguza kama mlezi anaweza kuripoti au kuonyesha uhusiano vizuri? mlezi anaongea, anauliza maswali, anacheza na vitu vya kuchezea, michezo ya maneno au mengineyo, ni sahihi kwa hatua ya maendeleo.
PONGEZA : Naona una fanya kazi nzuri kumfariji mtoto na unamuonesha upendo.

MSIFU mlezi kwa kufanya kazi nzuri ya kumrekebisha mtoto kwa njia chanya. Mhimize mlezi kumsifu mtoto kwa tabia zote nzuri. Naona unafanya kazi nzuri, kumbabaisha mtoto wako kutokana na tabia zisizo takiwa. Siku zote ni vizuri kutoa / kuondosha fikra za mtoto wako kumpeleka katika vitu au mambo chanya. Hii ni bora kwa maendeleo ya mtoto kuliko kumkemea au kumpiga. Kama mtoto atakuwa na tabia nzuri, mpongeze ili aendelea kuwa na tabia nzuri.
<span style="color:#f58a1f;font-weight:bold">MPONGEZE mlezi jinsi anavyoonyesha kucheza na mtoto kwa ajili ya maendeleo ya kiafya.
Tathmini Mlezi na Uhusiano wa mtoto na tabia

MSHAURI Kwamba umejaribu kadri ya uwezo wako kumfarijiri mtoto. Kwa kuongezea, nataka kuongezea kwamba ni muhimu ku mtizama mtoto machoni, kushikilia kwa utaratibu na kumsemesha kila wakati. Msaidie mama kuwasilisha tena kutumia mbinu mpya.
Je, mlezi amemfariji mtoto kwa kuangalia macho ya mtoto, na kuongea nae kwa upole, kumshika kwa taratibu au kumbeba?

Ningependa kuongea kuhusu namna ambavyo unatakiwa umliwaze mtoto na umuonyeshe upendo. Tujaalie mtoto anasumbuliwa na kitu sasa hivi. Unaweza kutuambia utafanya nini ili kumliwaza na kumuonyesha upendo?
Kwa maswali yafuatayo, ikiwezekana chunguza uhusiano kati ya mlezi na mtoto. Kama hujachunguza wakati wa tembeleo, muombe mlezi akuonyeshe.

MSHAURI jinsi ya kuboresha uhusiano kati ya mtoto na mlezi SEMA: Kucheza na kuwasiliana na mtoto wako ni muhimu katika kumsaidia kuwa na maendeleo mazuri kimwili, kiakili, lugha na mawasiliano, kijamii na kihisia, lakini huenda ikawa ni ngumu kujua unachotakiwa kufanya. Mtoto ambaye hana nafasi ya kucheza au kubadilishana sauti na tabasamu na walezi hawezi kuwa na maendeleo mazuri kimwili, kiakili, lugha na mawasiliano, kijamii na kihisia. Kwa muda mfupi, nitakupa baadhi ya mawazo kuhusiana na kucheza na kuwasiliana na mtoto wako.
Je, mlezi anaonyesha au anaelezea mwingiliano mzuri na mtoto kwa kuzungumza, kuimba, kupiga, kutabasamu, kucheza kwa upole na mdoli?
Je, unaweza kuniambia aina gani ya michezo, mazungumzo na nyimbo ambazo unafanya na mtoto wako, je unaweza kunionesha?
Je, mlezi alitaja njia za kumliwaza mtoto?
Mchunguze mlezi anavyoelezea uhusiano wake na mtoto.
MPONGEZE mlezi kwa kufanya kazi nzuri kumliwaza mtoto na kumuonesha upendo.
MPONGEZE mama kwa kucheza vizuri na mtoto: Ni vizuri kuona una jihusisha na mtoto wako kwa kuongea na kucheza! Nitakupa baadhi ya mawazo kuhusu kucheza na kuwasiliana na mtoto wako.
Je, unazungumza nae au kumwimbia mtoto wako au kujaribu kumfanya mtoto wako atabasamu?
Tathmini Mlezi na Uhusiano wa mtoto na tabia zake

Msifu baba kwa kutenga muda wa kuhudhuria kwenye mjadala wa tembeleo. Jadili na baba: Ufahamu wa mtoto wako hukua haraka katika miaka 3 ya kwanza ya maisha na uzoefu utakaopata wakati huu humsadia katika makuzi yake kwa maisha yake yote. Kadiri unavyoweza kuwasaidia watoto kukua kiakili wakati huu, ndivyo watakavyokuwa na akili na furaha zaidi shuleni na baadaye maishani. Familia ya mtoto inaweza kusaidia kwa: Kumfanya mtoto wako awe na afya njema kwa kumpeleka katika huduma ya chanjo na huduma ya kliniki haraka ikiwa mtoto wako ni mgonjwa. Katika Mpango Jamii ni Afya tutahakikisha kuwa watoto wako wanapata chanjo zote na kukusaidia kujifunza kutambua dalili za ugonjwa. Kumpa mtoto wako milo yenye afya. Milo ya mara kwa mara yenye aina tofauti za vyakula kama vile (Rejea Bangokitita ukurasa namba 30) ni muhimu ili kusaidia mwili na ubongo wa mtoto kukua vizuri. Katika Mpango Jamii ni Afya tutaangalia matatizo na ukuaji,na kuzungumza juu ya lishe bora za aina tofauti. Kusaidia ukuaji wa ubongo wa mtoto kwa kutenga muda kila siku Kucheza,Kuzungumza na Kumsifu mtoto wako. Mahusiano mazuri na watoto wachanga, watoto na wanafamilia huwatayarisha kwa mafanikio ya baadaye shuleni na maishani. Kwa upande mwingine, ugomvi mwingi, migogoro, dhiki au adhabu kali inaweza kuleta matatizo katika ukuaji wa mtoto. Katika matembeleo ya mpango wa Jamii ni Afya tutajifunza baadhi ya njia za kucheza, kuzungumza na kumsifu mtoto wako katika umri tofauti.

ULIZA: Waulize walezi kama wana maswali yoyote maalumu kuhusu jinsi ya kumsaidia na kumtunza mtoto wao ambaye amechelewa kukua. Waulize ni jambo gani gumu kwao siku hadi siku. Waulize kama wana malengo yoyote wanayotaka kufanyia kazi kwa mtoto wao. Pata maelezo zaidi na kutoa ushauri kuhusu kutunza watoto walio chelewa kukua na wenye ulemavu. Eleza kutokana na wasiwasi huu wa ukuaji wa mtoto wako, mwisho wa tembeleo hili nitakuelekeza kwenda kwenye kituo cha afya kilicho karibu nawe ili uweze kuwauliza kuhusu changamoto ya ukuaji wa mtoto wako na kupata huduma ya ziada ya afya kwa mtoto wako. CHUNGUZA: Uchelewaji wa kukua kwa mtoto au ulemavu, Maswali 11 ya uchunguzi yafuatayo. Jibu lolote la ndiyo linaonyesha hatari ya kuchelewa wa kukua au maendeleo ukuaji wa mtoto. Mshauri mama kwamba kwa sababu alionyesha wasiwasi kuhusu mtoto wake kuchelewa kujifunza, utauliza maswali zaidi ili kusaidia kuelewa ni maeneo yapi mtoto wako anaweza kuchelewa kukua ambazo zinaweza kuhitaji tathmini zaidi. Naomba nikuulize baadhi ya maswali maalumu yanayohusu maendeleo ya mtoto wako.
Miezi 12 hadi miaka 2: Kutembea mwenyewe. Majadiliano, na umri wa miaka 2 huanza kuunda sentensi fupi sana Anaanza kuunda maswali Anakula mwenyewe Kulala usiku mzima
Miaka 2 mpaka miaka 3: Anaanza kueleza anachokitaka, anaonesha vitu anavyovipenda, kuuliza maswali na kadhalika, anaweza kutunga sentensi nzima na anaanza kuhoji mambo yanayomzunguka.
Tabia za watoto kulingana na umri wao: Miezi 3 hadi 6: Husikiliza milio. Hutazama na kufuatiza macho kwenye vitu. Anapenda kuangalia watu usoni. Hutaka kuvishika vitu.
Miaka 4 mpaka miaka 5 : Anasimulia hadithi, anajua rangi na herufi kadhaa, na anaanza kuhesabu Wengine wanaweza kuelewa mtoto anapozungumza

Miezi 6 hadi 9: Unapojaribu kutabasamu na yeye anatabasamua. Huonesha ishara kama ana njaa. Anapiga kelele ili kuwasiliana. Anaweza kukaa kitako mwenyewe. Huwa anamtambua na anaitika kwa mlezi wake na pia anapenda kuona rangi tofauti.
Miezi 9 hadi 12: Anaanza kuzungumza na kuunda maneno ya kwanza Anajibu kwa walezi kwa maneno na kimwili Anaanza kutambaa Anasimama mwenyewe Kutembea mwenyewe au kwa msaada
Mtoto ambaye si mwepesi wa kujifunza anaweza kuhuzunisha na kuleta msongo wa mawazo kwa kila mtu katika familia.
Je, una wasiwasi wowote kuhusu jinsi mtoto anavyoona au kusikia?
Je, mtoto ana matatizo yoyote kwamba mtoto hajibu au inaonekana kuwa mgumu wa kujifunza?

Zungumza na mlezi ikiwa ni pamoja na: Mtoto aliyechelewa kukua(Mdumavu) anaweza kukua na kujifunza Ni muhimu mtoto apate huduma ya afya ya mara kwa mara kama vile chanjo na kutembelea kliniki wakati anaumwa. Mtoto aliyechelewa kukua (Mdumavu) anaweza kuhitaji usaidizi zaidi wa kulishwa lakini apewe milo yenye afya na ya kawaida. Mtoto aliyechelewa kukuaa anaweza kuhitaji usaidizi zaidi wa kukaa, kuoga na kujisaidia. Kutumia muda wa ziada kucheza na kuwasiliana na mtoto wako inaweza kumsaidia kukuza ujuzi mpya Mshirikishe mtoto wako katika shughuli zako za kila siku kama vile kwenda dukani au kula chakula na familia Jaribu kutambua na kuhimiza ujuzi alionao mtoto
Ni umri gani unawiana na maendeleo ya mtoto?
Je, hii ni mara ya kwanza kwa baba kushiriki kwenye mjadala wa tembeleo?
Ikiwa baba hakuhudhuria, Uliza kama baba amewahi kushiriki kwenye matembeleo ya siku zilizopita. Ikiwa hapana, mwambie mama ajaribu kupanga ratiba ili baba ahudhurie kwenye matembeleo ya siku zijazo.
Tumia uwiano wa umri huu kuchagua na kufundisha kuhusu shughuli za michezo na mawasiliano inayo wiana na hatua za ukuaji wa mtoto.
**_Angalia Hatua za Maendeleo ya mtoto ili kusaidia shughuli za uchezaji na mawasiliano zilizopo karibu zaidi na hatua ya ukuaji wa mtoto_**
Ukilinganisha na watoto wengine. je, mtoto wako anaonekana ni mzito kiakili, si mchangamfu au ni mgumu kujifunza?
Je, mtoto wako anaweza kuita jina la kitu japo kimoja? (kwa mfano, mnyama, mdoli, kikombe au kijiko)
Je, mtoto wako anajifunza kufanya baadhi vitu kama vile wanavyofanya watoto wengine wa umri wake?
Ukilinganisha na watoto wengine. je, mtoto wako ana tatizo lolote linalohusiana na kuchelewa kukaa, kusimama au kutembea?
Je, mtoto wako ana tatizo la kusikia? (anatumia kifaa kinachomsaidia kusikia, anasikia kwa shida, hasikii kabisa)
Ukilinganisha na watoto wengine. je, mtoto wako ana tatizo la kutokuona nyakati za usiku au machana?.
Je, mtoto wako anapata shida wakati wa kutembea au anapojaribu kuzungusha mikono yake au Je, ana udhaifu wowote kwenye miguu na mikono?
Je, mtoto wako anaweza kuzungumza? Je, anaweza kuzungumza maneno yanayoeleweka?
Je, mtoto wako wakati mwingine ana degedege/kukukutika/mdudu, inakuwa ngumu, au kupoteza fahamu?
Je, mtoto wako anazungumza tofauti na inavyopaswa kuwa? (mazungumzo yake hayaeleweki vizuri na watu wengine isipokuwa watu wa karibu wa familia yake)
Unapomwambia mtoto wako afanye kitu fulani. je, anaonekana anakuelewa ulichomwambia?

Mshauri mlezi kwamba ungependa kuzungumza kuhusu tabia na ukuaji wa mtoto na uangalie jinsi wanavyoshirikiana pamoja. Eleza kwamba jinsi mtoto anavyokua na kujifunza inategemea sana tabia ambayo walezi huonyesha kwa mtoto. Je, kuna wanafamilia wengine au walezi ili washiriki katika mjadala huu unaohusu maendeleo ya mtoto? Hakikisha unamuhamasisha baba kushiriki kama yupo, wakaribishe ili washiriki kwenye mjadala, pia muombe mama awe na mtoto wake karibu. Wakaribishe walezi na familia na wahamasishe kushiriki kwenye mjadala.
Nani ameshiriki kwenye mjadala huu wa tembeleo nyumbani?
Matunzo kwa ajili ya Maendeleo ya Mtoto. (CCD): Tathmini maendeleo na fundisha kuhusu mawasiliano na michezo
Je mtoto amewahi kupata degedege kwa siku za karibuni?
Mtoto wako ana dalili za hatari hizi zinahitaji kushungulikiwa kwa haraka. Mpeleke mtoto kituo cha afya kilichopo karibu. Ikiwa kituo kimefungwa nenda hospitali iliyopo karibu nawe.
Nihitaji kumchunguza mtoto wako dalili za hatari. Je, mwanao ameonesha dalili zote kati ya hizi katika masaa 24 yaliyo pita?
Je mtoto anapumua kwa tabu au kwa haraka (anapumua mara 60 au zaidi kwa daikika moja)?
Je mtoto an homa au joto kali?
Je mtoto analegea na kupoteza fahamu?
Je mtoto anaharisha sana? (Kupata choo laini sana cha maji maji zaidi ya mara 10 ndani ya masaa 24)

Sasa hivi upo kwenye hatua ya kuchunguza dalili za hatari kwa mtoto huyu. Tafadhali epuka kumshika mtoto ili kukulinda kutokana na uwezako wa kuambukizwa virusi vya korona. Ikiwa kuna ulazima wa kumgusa ili kujua hali ya mtoto tafadhali muombe mteja afanye hivyo kwa niaba yako.
Je mtoto anaona baridi au ana kiwango cha chini cha joto?
Je mtoto anaweza kunywa au kunyonya maziwa ya mama?
Je mtoto anaweza kula na kunywa?
Je mtoto anaweza kunyonya maziwa ya mama?
Je mtoto anaharisha? (Kupata choo laini sana cha maji maji chini ya mara 10 ndani ya masaa 24)
Dalili ya hatari imegundulika. Mshauri mama aende kituo cha Afya kilicho karibu nae ndani ya masaa 24 yajayo ili Daktari aweze kumuangalia mtoto na kutoa msaada.
Je mtoto ana majipu katika ngozi?
Dalili nyengine za hatari za mtoto
Hakuna dalili ya hatari yoyote iliyoonekana kwa mtoto wako, lakini ikitokea mtoto ameonesha dalili yoyote ya hatari tulizojadili, hakikisha unamuwahisha kituo cha afya kilichopo karibu nawe haraka.

Ningependa tujadili jinsi ya kukihifadhi vinyesi vya Watoto, kinyesi cha mtoto kinaweza kuwa hatari au chanzo cha maradhi.
Ni wapi mtoto wako anajisaidia? Ni wapi unapokitupa kinyesi cha mtoto wako?
Choo cha shimo kisicho na paipu
Choo chenye kikalio ambacho kina paipu
Kutumia choo inaweza kusadia kukuepusha na usambaaji wa maradhi. Ikiwa upo uwezekano, ni vyema ukawa na choo ndani mwako.

Mueleze mama hatari ya kinyesi cha mtoto na njia salama ya kukihifadhi: Njia salama ya kukihifadhi kinyesi cha mtoto ni kumsaidia mtoto aweze kujisaidia chooni au kukitupa kinyesi cha watoto wachanga kwenye shimo la choo. Upo ushahidi unaoonesha kuwa kinyesi cha mtoto ni hatari zaidi kuliko cha mtu mzima. Vinyesi vya watoto ni vyema vikahifadhiwa kama vya watu wazima, kutumia njia salama ya kuvitupa ambayo itahakikisha watu hawakutani navyo au havileti uchafuzi kwenye kaya.

Habari mama? Kama tulivyozungumza wakati wa matembeleo yetu yaliyopita kuwa ntakuja kukutembelea kwa ajili ya kujua maendeleo na afya ya mtoto/watoto, na pia kujadiliana na wewe pamoja na familia juu ya malezi na makuzi bora ya mtoto/watoto wako. Nafahamu kuwa unajua jinsi ya kumtunza mtoto wako na kumkuza vizuri, lakini hii ni kama nyongeza tu ya elimu kutokana na ongezeko la maradhi na matatizo mbali mbali yanayoweza kudhoofisha afya na makuzi bora ya mtoto.
Tarehe ya mwisho ya tembeleo:
Jina: Umri: miaka, miezi, siku Mtu wa karibu kwa mawasiliano: Namba ya nyumba: Kitongoji: .

Maelekezo kwa CHV: Chagua mchezo mmoja (1) na shughuli za mawasiliano moja (1) kutoka katika mwongozo wa mchezo na mawasiliano katika hatua za maendeleo ya mtoto. i. Waoneshe wazazi picha na elezea kila shughuli. ii. Wacha mlezi ajaribu hizo shughuli. Chunguza uhusiano na msaidie mlezi kuwa na hisia na ukaribu kwa ishara za mtoto. Lengo ni kumsaidia mlezi na mtoto kuwa na mafanikio kwenye uhusiano wa pande zote mbili na mlezi kuchunguza, kusikia na kujihusisha na mtoto.] iii. Kama mtoto hawezi kufanya hizo shughuli chagua shughuli kutoka kwenye miaka midogo au pendekeza njia ya kumsaidia mtoto kujifunza vitu vipya au chagua shughuli katika umri mkubwa Zaidi zifuatazo. iv. Himiza mlezi kujihusisha katika michezo na shughuli za mawasiliano kila siku.

Watoto hujifunza kwa kuona kile ambacho wengine hufanya, kuiga na kufanya mazoezi ya mambo mapya. Kucheza ni njia nzuri ya kuwasaidia watoto kujifunza na kufanya mazoezi ya ujuzi mpya! Kadiri tunavyoweza kutumia wakati mwingi kucheza na watoto wetu, ndivyo habari zaidi inavyotolewa kwa ubongo wao ili kuusaidia kukua. Pia huwasaidia kimwili. Waache watoto wakuongoze jinsi wanavyotaka kucheza, na kufuata maslahi yao. Kuzungumza na watoto wetu, kuitikia sauti na ishara zao, na kuwasaidia kujifunza kutaja vitu husaidia ubongo wao kukua na kuwasaidia kujifunza kuzungumza.
Mwambie mlezi kuwa utamuonyesha jinsi ya kufanya mazoezi ya kucheza na kuwasiliana ili aendelee kufanya nyumbani.
Muulize mtoto wako masuali rahisi. Itika kwa mazungumzo anayojaribu kuongea mtoto wako. Muoneshe na uzungumze kuhusu asili, picha na vitu.

Muhimize mtoto wako kuongea na kujibu maswali anayoulizwa. Mfundishe mtoto wako hadithi, nyimbo na michezo. Ongea kuhusu picha au kitabu. Mtoto atajifunza maneno ikiwa utachora na kurudia maneno. MSHAURI mlezi kwamba wanaweza kuchora picha mzuri kwenye madaftari ya watoto wao wenyewe, sio lazima wanunue dukani. Kwa mfano: Kitabu cha picha mambo ya kuchora ,kengele, pembea, baiskeli.
Tabasamu na ucheke na mtoto wako. Zungumza na mtoto wako. Fanya mazungumzo na mtoto wako kwa kuiga sauti yake na vitendo vyake

Muhimize mtoto wako kuhesabu, kulinganisha na kufananisha vitu vilivyo mzunguka. Mpe fursa ya kuchora. Msaidie kujifunza herufi na nambari. Muhimize kukusaudua kwenye kazi au shughuli za nyumbani. Mpe fursa kukimbia, kuruka na kupanda.
Itika juu ya sauti ya mtoto wako na uwe na hamu nae. Muite kwa jina mtoto wako na uone kama ataitika.
Mwambie mtoto wako jina la vitu na watu. Muoneshe mtoto wako jinsi ya kusema kwa kupitia mikono, kama "kwaheri" sampuli ya vitu: doli

Muhimize mtoto wako kuhesabu, kulinganisha na kufananisha vitu vilivyo mzunguka. Mpe fursa ya kuchora. Msaidie kujifunza herufi na nambari. Muhimize kukusaudua kwenye kazi au shughuli za nyumbani. Mpe fursa kukimbia, kuruka na kupanda.
Msaidie mtoto wako kuhesabu, kutaja na kulinganisha vitu. Tengeneza vitu rahisi kwa mtoto wako, Vitu vya sampuli, vitu vilivyo na rangi tofauti tofauti na maumbo kupanga na kushikanisha.
Mpe mtoto wako vitu visafi na salama vya kubeba, kugonga na kuangusha. Vitu vya sampuli: chombo kilicho na mfuniko, masufuria na kijiko.
Ficha kitu cha kuchezea anachokipenda mtoto wako katika nguo au boksi. Angalia kama mtoto wako atakipata. cheza mchezo wakujificha.
Mpe mtoto vitu vya kupanga na kuingiza katika chombo na kutoa. vitu vya sampuli: kuweka na kupanga vitu, chombo na kukunja nguo.
Baadhi ya njia nzuri za kuwasiliana na mtoto wako ni:
Katika umri huu, baadhi ya njia nzuri za kucheza na mtoto wako ni:
Kufundisha kucheza na shughuli za kuwasiliana

Kwa vile nilishamsajili mtoto kwenye huduma za afya za mtoto, leo nitaangalia afya yake, na nitakuwa narudia kumtembelea kadiri anavyoendelea kukuwa ili kuhakikisha kuwa anakuwa na afya bora. Je mnakubaliana na hili?
Isalimie familia na waalike wengine waliopo washiriki kwenye mazungumzo ikiwa inahitajika.

Kama mhudumu wa afya ya jamii, una jukumu la kuhakikisha unatoa elimu ya afya ya jamii na huduma huku ukijikinga ili kuepusha kusambaa kwa maambukizi ya korona kutoka kwa mtu mmoja kwenda kwa mwengine. Hivyo hakikisha unazingatia yafuatayo wakati ukiwa unatoa huduma: • Osha mikono yako kwa maji na sabuni au kutumia vitakasa mikono mara nyingi kadiri unavyoweza ukiwa nyumbani kwako,nyumbani kwa mteja wako na pindi ukimaliza kutoa huduma. • Hakikisha kuna umbali wa mita mbili kati yako na mteja wako unayeenda kumtembelea • Pindi ukiongea na wateja wako , usishikane mikono. • Epuka mikusanyiko. • Toa huduma katika maeneo yaliyo wazi na ikitokea ukafanya ndani hakikisha milango na madirisha yanakuwa wazi. • Funika mdomo wako kwa kitambaa au kiwiko unavyooenda chafya au kukohoa. • Tafadhali usiende kutoa huduma ukijihisi na dalili za homa na kukohoa na kwenda chafya kwa wingi. • Tafadhali kumbuka kufungua fomu ya kuwaelimsiha wanakaya juu ya maambukizi ya korona na jinsi ya kuchukua tahadhari. Tembeleo kuhusu korona unaweza kulifanya kabla ya kuanza tembeleo lako au mara baada ya kumaliza tembeleo lako hili.
Hatua za kuchukua pindi unapoenda kumtembelea mteja wako

Eleza yafuatayo kuhusu umuhimu wa kumnyonyesha mtoto njiti angalau kwa masaa 2-3: Maziwa ya mama ni chakula bora sana kwa mtoto njiti kwani humkinga na maambukizi. Watoto njiti wanatakiwa mwanyonyeshwe mara kwa mara kwasababu huwa wananyonya kidogo tu. Ikiwa mtoto atalala zaidi ya masaa 2 , muamshe kwa kugusagusa kwenye viganja au migunii ili umnyonyeshe hata nyakati za usiku.
Kwa mujibu wa tathmini yako na maelezo uliyoyapata kutoka kwa mama, Je mama anamyonyesha mtoto vizuri?
zaidi ya mara tano kwa siku
Ni kwa kiasi gani unamnyonyesha mtoto wako, Ikiwemo usiku na mchana?

ULIZA na CHUNGUZA: Je vipi mtoto anahifadhiwa? Mpongeze mlezi ikiwa mtoto huwa anafunikwa vizuri na kuwa karibu na mama yake, na ikiwa anakoshwa kwa maji ya vuguvugu na kukaushwa haraka. Ikiwa sio, jaribu kutatua tatizo. Angalia ukurasa wa 10 wa Bango Kitita kwa melezo zaidi.

Je unaendeleaje na kunyonyesha? Je unamuekaje mtoto ukitaka kumnyonyesha? Unanyonyesha kila baada ya kipindi gani? Mtoto anachukua muda gani kunyonya kwa mara moja? SIKILIZA: Mruhusu mama kueleza uzoefu wake wa kunyonyesha.
Ningependa kuzungumza na wewe kuhusu unyonyeshaji wa mtoto. ULIZA: Je, umeshawahi kunyonyesha?
Je mtoto ananyonyeshwa maziwa ya mama peke yake?
Zungumza na mama na jaribu kujua kwanini hampi mwanawe mazi ya mama pekee. Muelimishe kwamba ni muhimu kutokumlisha mwanawe kitu chengine chochote isipukuwa maziwa ya mama.
Hakuna kimoja wapo kati ya hivyo.
Huwezi kuchaguwa "Hakuna moja wapo" na chaguo jengine
Je mtoto amekula au kunywa kitu chochote kati ya hivi vifuatavyo jana?
Je una tatizo lolote unahitaji tujadili? Jadili tatizo na mama.

Zungumza na mama kuhusu tatizo na jaribu kulitatuwa. Wacha tujifunze jinsi ya kumueka mtoto vizuri mtoto wakati wa kumnyonyesha. Je unaweza kunionesha jinsi gani unamueka mtoto wako? Msaidie mama kuboresha jinsi ya kumueka mtoto: muombe amgusishe mtoto ncha ya ziwa kwenye midomo yake, subiria hadi mtoto afungue mdomo wake vizuri, na muingizie mtoto ziwa karibu na mdomo wa chini.
Zungumza na mama na jaribu kujua kwanini hamnyonyeshi mwanawe zaidi ya mara 5 kwa siku. Jaribu kumuelimisha na kumsaidia kutatua tatizo hili.

CHUNGUZA: kama kuna uwezekano muombe mama amnyonyeshe mtoto wake na chunguza, ikiwa hakuna uwezekano muulize mama maswali kuliko kumchunguza. Osha mikono yako. CHUNGUZA Je mama anayenyonyesha anaelewa hili ? Je umegundua kuna tatizo lolote kwenye kunyonyesha? Toa muongozo kwa kile ulichokiona. Jaribu kutatua tatizo lolote uliloliona au alilolisema mama.
Mpongeze mama ikiwa anampa mtoto maziwa ya mama pekee.

Anza mazungumzo kwa kuelezea mtoto njiti. Mtoto njiti ni mototo ambaye anapozaliwa anakuwa na uzito wa chini ya kilo mbili na kwahivyo uzito wake ni chini ya uzito unaotakiwa. Watoto njiti kikawaida huwa na matatizo mengi ya kiafya ukilinganisha na waotto wengine. Kwa hivyo ni muhimu kugundua dalili za hatari za mtoto njiti na namna gani ya kukabiliana na dalili hizo.
Baada ya kujifungua, ni muda gani ulichukua kuanza kumnyonyesha mtoto wako?
Huduma muhimu ya watoto wachanga: Kuhifadhi na kunyonyesha
Muelimishe mama njia nzuri za upakataji na uwekaji wa mtoto katika ziwa ili kufanikisha unyonyrshaji. Fungua bango kitita kadi namba 16 na 17.

Saivi upo katika maeneo ya kumfundisha mlezi jinsi ya kumpakata mtoto wake ili aweze kunyonya vizuri, hili huwa linahitaji maingiliano ya moja kwa moja kati yako na mteja na pengine hulazimu kumshika mtoto ili kumuelekeza vizuri. Ingawa kwa kipindi cha mpito unapaswa kuepuka maingiliano ya moja kwa moja na mteja na epuka kumshika mtoto. Hii ni kwaajili ya kuepusha uwezekano wa kueneza maradhi ya korona. Tafadhali muelekeze mama cha kufanya huku ukiwa unachunguza.

Je, upakataji na uwekaji wa mtoto katika titi uko sawa? Hii inamaanisha kuwa kidevu kinagusa titi,kinywa cha mtoto kimeachama sawasawa, mdomo wa chini wa mtoto unabinuka kwa nje, sehemu kubwa nyeusi ya titi inaonekana zaidi juu ya kinywa cha mtoto kuliko chini.

Msaidie Mama kufanya haya yafuatayo: Kukaa au kulala vizuri. Mshike mtoto mwili mzima, sio shingo tu na mabega. Hakikisha kichwa cha mtoto na mwili vimenyooka. Mtoto atazame ziwa la mama. Pua ya mtoto iwe mkabala na kinyonyeo. Alishike ziwa na mgusishe mtoto nalo kwenye mdomo wake ili mtoto afungue mdomo. Hakikisha mdomo wa mtoto umekaa vizuri kwenye ncha ya ziwa la mama. Hakikisha mtoto amewekwa vyema ili kuweza kunyonya vizuri. Agusishe mdomo wa mtoto na kinyonyeo.
Mpongeze mama kwa kumuweka vizuri mtoto wake wakati wa kumnyonyesha.

Msaidie mama kujua Zaidi kumuweka vizuri mtoto kwa ajili ya kumnyonyesha. Muongozo wa kumuweka mtoto vizuri wakati wa kunyonya: Kama mtoto analala sana na anakosa kunyonya, muamshe kwa kupapasa mwili wake, miguu au mikono. Ikiwa mtoto bado hanyonyi baada ya saa moja, kamua maji maji ya mwanzo ya njano kutoka kwenye maziwa kwenye kikombe au kijiko kilicho safi. Mpe mtoto anywe mara kwa mara na kwa kiwango kidogo, hakikisha mtoto anaweza kumeza. Kugusisha midomo ya mtoto kwa kinyonyeo chake. Asubiri hadi mdomo wa mtoto umefunguka sana. Amuweke mtoto haraka kwenye maziwa akitega mdomo wa chini wa mtoto vizuri chini ya kinyonyeo. Kama maziwa yamevimba, pakaza kiwango kidogo cha maziwa kabla ya kuanza kumnyonyesha mtoto ili kulainisha kinyonyeo na iwe rahisi kwa mtoto kunyonya.
Mpongeze mama na mshauri kuendelea kufanya hivyo.
Huduma muhimu ya watoto wachanga: Upakataji na uwekaji wa mtoto katika titi.

Usafi ni muhimu wakati wa chakula cha ziada, mshauri hivi mama: Usafi ni muhimu huzuiya mtoto kuharisha. Tumia kjiko na kikombe safi wakati wa kumlisha na kumnywesha mtoto. Kosha mikono yako kwa sabuni na maji yanaotiririka mara kwa mara.
Usafi ni muhimu wakati wa chakula cha ziada, mshauri hivi mama: Usafi ni muhimu huzuiya mtoto kuharisha. Kosha mikono yako kwa sabuni na maji yanaotiririka mara kwa mara.

Sasa naomba tuongee kuhusu usafi wa chakula: Usafi ni mzuri na muhimu kwa kujikinga na maradhi ya kuharisha. Tumia kijiko au kikombe safi wakati wa kumlisha au kumnywesha mtoto. Kosha mikono yako kwa sabuni na maji yanayotiririka mara kwa mara, hasa kabla ya kuanza kumlisha mtoto.
Ikiwa mtoto wako hanyonyi tena, tafadhali hakikisha anapata chakula chenye afya na kilicho bora.

Endeleae kumnyonyesha mtoto wako kwa mujibu wa mahitaji yake muda wote, usiku na mchana. Hili litafanya afya ya mtoto kutengemaa na kumfanya awe na nguvu, kwani maziwa ya mama ni chakula muhimu sana kwa mtoto.

Jaribu kupika vyakula tofauti tofauti kwa ajili ya mtoto: Kila wiki unaweza kuongeza aina mpya ya chakula kwa mtoto. Mlishe mtoto chakula cha ziada mara mbili kwa siku. Mpe mtoto ladha tofauti mara mbili au tatu kwenye kila mlo. Maziwa ya mama yabaki kuwa ni chakula kikuu kwenye umri huu wa mtoto. Endelea kunyonyesha kwa mujibu wa mahitaji ya mtoto. Kunywa maji masafi na ya kawaida ni kitu kizuri kwa afya ya mtoto. Mlishe mtoto kwenye chombo cha peke yake.
Matunzo muhimu ya mtoto mdogo -- Lishe

Kikawaida kamba iliyopo kwenye kitovu cha mtoto wako hukauka na kudondoka chenyewe ndani ya wiki moja ua mbili baada ya mtoto kuzaliwa. Katika kipindi hiki hakikisha unakitunza kipaswavyo: Hakikisha kinakuwa kikavu: jaribu kukiruhusu kipate hewa ili iwe rahisi kukauka. Unapomfunga mtoto wako hakikisha unamfunga kwa chini ya kitovu ili kuepuka kukifunika. Usiingize kitu chochote kwenye kitovu cha mtoto_unapoeka kitu chochote inaweza kusababisha madhara kwa mtoto. Muogeshe kwa kumfuta. Kumuogesha kwa kumfuta ni vizuri, itasaidia kuafanya kitovu kikauke haraka. Iache kamba ya kitovu idondoke yenyewe epuka kujaribu kuivuta ili kidondoke.
Huduma muhimu kwa watoto wachanga: Jinsi ya kukitunza kitovu.
[Siku 0-7:] Ongea na mwimbie mtoto. Mhamasishe aweze kuangalia, kusikiliza, kurusha miguu na mikono na kugusa vitu. Muoneshe tabasamu. Mshike mtoto wa upendo.

[Siku 8 hadi miezi 6]: MCHEZO: Toa njia za mtoto wako kuangalia, kusikiliza, kurusha mikono na miguu kwa uhuru, na kukugusa. Pole pole msogezee mtoto wako vitu vya rangi kwa ajili ya kuviangalia na kuvifuatilia. Mfano: Vitu vya kuchezea: kitairi kikubwa kwenye uzi, mwiko. WASILIANA: Tabasamu na cheka. Zungumza na mtoto wako. Hojiana kwa kuiga sauti ya mtoto wako au ishara.

Wewe na mwenzako/ mume wako au familia muna jukumu la kuhakikisha maendeleo ya akili ya mtoto na katika kipindi hiki cha miezi na miaka ya mwanzoni ni vyema kutumiwa vyema ili kuifanya akili yake ikuwe vizuri
Mtoto mchanga anaweza kuona vitu, kuvihisi, na anaweza kujibu sauti na milio huwa anasikia. Mtoto anaweza kutuambia kuwa anaona njaa au hana furaha kwa kwa sauti yake. Mtoto huwa anapenda kuona sura ya mama na baba yake (walezi wake wa karibu.)[ angalia tafsiri kwenye muongozo wa kazi]
Kadiri tunavyowapa nafasi watoto wetu kudadisi wanayaona (kwa kucheza, kufanya mambo mapya, kuona vitu vipya na kuiga) ndio kadiri tunavyoweza kuwafanya wawe na akili.
Kwa mtoto wako unaweza :
Huduma muhimu/za lazima kwa mtoto mchanga: Kuchangamsha
Je uzito wa mtoto alipozaliwa umeandikwa kwenye kadi?
Lengo ni kukagua kama mtoto amezaliwa na uzito mdogo au la!
Ingiza uzito wa mtoto kwa Kilo.
Kwa kukisia mtoto aliazaliwa na ukubwa wa kiasi gani?
Iangalie kadi ya mtoto au Kijitabu cha huduma za mtoto njiti ili kupanga matembeleo ya ufatiliaji. Mshauri mama arudi kituo cha afya kwa matembeleo ya ufatiliaji.
Je umepatiwa huduma maalumu wanayopatiwa watoto waliozaliwa wadogo hospitali?
Huu ni ukubwa wa kawaida wa mtoto kwahivyo anahitaji apate uangalizi wa kawaida. Utajifaunza ni jinsi gani tunapaswa kutunza watoto waliozaliwa na ukubwa wa kawaida baadae.
Mpe rufaa kwenye wodi ya watoto njiti.
Mtoto huyu amezaliwa mdogo hivyo anahitaji uangalizi wa ziada kutoka kwa wahudumu wa afya wa jamii wa kujitolea na familia. Tutazungumza kuhusu uangalizi maalumu anaopaswa kupatiwa mtoto wako.
anaaendelea vyema na hahitaji kwenda kituo cha Afya kwa sasa. Utarudi kumkagua tena wakati wa tembeleo jengine.
Ili kushughulikia dalili za hatari za dharura za mtoto.
Ili kushughulikia dalili za hatari za mtoto.
Ili kushughulikia maswala ya kujifunza na maendeleo ya mtoto.
Ili kushughulikia dalili za hatari za dharura za mtoto mchanga.
anahitaji kupelekwa kituo cha Afya kwa sababu zifuatazo:
Ili kushughulikia dalili za hatari za mtoto mchanga.
Apate huduma maalum kwa ajili ya watoto wadogo.
Umekataa kupata huduma ya Afya ya jamii. Sitofanya tembeleo lolote linalohusu Afya ya jamii kwake .
Wahamasishe wazazi kumpeleka mtoto kituo cha Afya haraka iwezekanavyo ili kupata huduma. Wataarifu kwamba utarejea baada ya siku tatu kuhakikisha kwamba wameweza kupata huduma stahiki kituo cha Afya.
Sasa nitapendelea kupitia kadi la kliniki la mtoto kuhakikisha amepata/wamepata chanjo zote stahiki mpaka sasa. Naomba unioneshe kadi ya mtoto wako.
Muombe mama ahakikishe kuwa wahudumu wa kituo cha afya wanaandika rikodi kuhusu chanjo kwenye kadi la afya la mtoto.
Kabla ya kuomba kadi la kliniki la mtoto, hakikisha unanawa mikono yako vizuri kwa maji na sabuni na pia muombe mteja aoshe mikono yake kabla ya kukukabidhi wewe kadi hiyo.
Naomba uniitie Mumeo au wanafamilia ili nipate kuwaeleza umuhimu wa chanjo.
Sasa nitapendelea kupitia kadi la kliniki la mtoto kuhakikisha amepata/wamepata chanjo zote stahiki mpaka sasa. Je, Una kadi la kliniki la mtoto wako?

Katika hatua hii mtoto wako anapaswa kupatiwa chanjo, Hakikisha unaenda kituo cha afya ili apatiwe. Ikitokea hakupatiwa kutokana na sababu zisizoepukika, hakikisha unamrudisha tena Kituoni ili apatiwe chanjo.Hii ni muhimu sana kwa afya ya mtoto wako
Tarehe ya chanjo iwe baina ya tarehe ya kuzaliwa mtoto na tarehe ya leo.

Kawaida mtoto akishachomwa sindano ya chanjo ya BCG inabidi baada ya miezi mitatu kovu ionekanwe kwenye bega la kulia la mtoto. Kama hutoona kovu imetokea, mshauri mama ampeleke mtoto kituo cha Afya na kuwaambia watoa huduma waliopo kituo cha Afya ili apatiwe chanjo tena.

Watoto wote chini ya miaka mitano wanaweza kupatiwa chanjo zote, hata kama muda wa chanjo umepita. Tafadhali muwahishe mtoto wako kituo cha afya kwa ajili ya kupatiwa chanjo.
Mpongeze mama na mkumbushe kuhusu tarehe ya chanjo nyengine kama kadi ilinavyoonesha. mkumbushe kuwa kuna umuhimu mkubwa ikiwa mtoto wake atapatiwa chanjo zote kwa muda uliopangwa.

Ikiwa mtoto wako analia kwa zaidi ya masaa 3 au ukiwa unahofu jinsi anavyooneka au anavyojiskia, nenda kituo cha afya haraka. Ni mara chache sana kujitokeza kwa athari yoyote mbaya. Lakini inaweza kujitokeza baadhi ya muda mtu anapotumia dawa yoyote ile.

Baada ya kupata chanjo, mtoto wako anaweza kupata homa, upele au maumivu kwenye mkono au mguu katika maeneo ambayo amechomwa sindano. Mnyonyeshe mtoto wako. Hii hali ni ya kawaida kujitokeza wala sio tatizo ikiwa hali hii itachelewa kuondoka au una shaka yoyote nenda kituo cha afya na ufate ushauri wataokupatia.
Toa ushauri nasaha kuhusu umuhimu wa chanjo na mpe rufaa aende kituo cha afya.
Washauri kuhusu ratiba ya chanjo kituoni na wape taarifa kuhusu upatikanaji wake, jitahidi uhakikishe kuwa mama anahudhuria kwenye chanjo siku iliyopangwa.
Ikiwa kuna umuhimu, mkumbushe mama kuwa chanjo zote ni muhimu kwa ajiili ya kumkinga mtoto na maradhi kama vile kuharisha, polio,surua, Kifua kikuu, nimonia, na mengineyo.

Mwenza hakuonesha ushirikiano, hakuuliza maswali au anaonekana hayaafiki maelezo.
Mwenza ameuliza maswali machache na ameonekana kuzingatia na kufurahishwa na maelezo.
Mwenza ameuliza maswali mengi na ameonekana kuzingatia na kufurahishwa na maelezo.

Mtoto wako ana uzito pungufu hakikisha unamuwahisha kituo cha afya kilichopo karibu na wewe ukamuone daktari ili ampime mtoto wako?
Kabla ya kuomba kitabu cha mtoto cha makuzi, hakikisha unanawa mikono yako vizuri kwa maji na sabuni na pia muombe mteja aoshe mikono yake kabla ya kukukabidhi wewe kitabu hicho.
Maziwa au bidhaa kama mtindi au jibini iliyotengenezwa kwa maziwa.
Mikate ya mchele au vyakula vingine vinavyotengenezwa kwa nafaka au viazi mbatata, Muhogo

Aina yoyote ya nyama ,samaki, Maini, mapafu.

Tazama kitabu cha mtoto cha makuzi. Ingiza makadirio ya grafu ambayo mtoto yupo.
Ni milo mingapi ya chakula kikavu au cha maji maji unapaswa kumlisha mtoto wako kila siku?
Mpime MUAC na onesha kama je yupo kwenye kijani, manjano au nyekundu.

Kutokana na kipimo cha lishe cha mtoto wako kinaonesha ana utapia mlo mkali. Ili kukusaidia mtoto wako kurudia katika hali yake bora ya lishe, nakupatia rufaa na nitarudia siku chache kufatilia kuhusu rufaa.

Mtoto yupo katika hatari ya utapia mlo
Mtoto wako anahitaji uangalizi wa ziada ili kumuepusha na utapia mlo. Nitapokuja kukutembelea tena tutajadili kuhusu lishe na kipi cha kufanya ili kupata lishe b

Kiganja ni cha njano au kina mpauko
Sasa nitaangalia wingi wa damu wa mtoto wako.
Je kiganja chake cha mkono ni cheupe sana? Linganisha kiganja cha mama na cha mtoto.
Ili kukusaidia mtoto wako apate matibabu, nitahakikisha nitampa rufaa ya wa tatizo la upungufu wa damu.

Sasa hivi upo kwenye hatua ya kuchunguza dalili za hatari kwa mtoto huyu. Tafadhali epuka kumshika mtoto ili kukulinda kutokana na uwezako wa kuambukizwa virusi vya korona. Ikiwa kuna ulazima wa kumgusa ili kujua hali ya mtoto tafadhali muombe mteja afanye hivyo kwa niaba yako.
Kuchunguza Dalili za hatari kwa mtoto mchanga
Mtoto wako ana dalili za hatari hizi zinahitaji kushungulikiwa kwa haraka. Mpeleke mtoto kituo cha afya kilichopo karibu. Ikiwa kituo kimefungwa nenda hospitali iliyopo karibu nawe.
Je mtoto amebadilika rangi kuwa buluu,njano au zambarau?
Muelimishe mama jinsi ya kutambua dalili za hatari kwa mtoto mchanga na umuhimu wa kutafuta msaada wa matibabu katika kituo cha afya haraka. Tumia bango kitita kadi namba 18.

Nahitaji kumchunguza mtoto wako dalili za hatari. Iwapo mtoto ataonesha ana dalili za hatari, atapatiwa rufaa (kwenda kituo cha afya) na kuunganishwa na mtoa huduma kwenye kituo kilicho karibu naye. Je, mwanao ameonesha dalili zote kati ya hizi katika masaa 24 yaliyo pita?
